channel.title: Ninad Pathak
channel.description: Programmer-turned-writer helping DevTools and SaaS companies create technical content their engineering teams actually approve.

item.title: Why Voice AI Agents Break Standard Memory Architectures
item.description: Voice AI agents aren't just text chatbots with a microphone attached. The real-time, turn-taking, interruption-heavy nature of spoken dialogue breaks every assumption that works for text-based memory systems. Here's what I learned building both.

item.title: AI Memory Management for LLMs: How Modern AI Systems Remember (and Forget)
item.description: A technical deep-dive into memory management strategies for LLMs and AI agents — covering KV cache eviction, importance-weighted retention, and production systems like Letta and MemGPT.

item.title: How Memory Works in DeerFlow: A Deep Dive into the 2026 Agent Harness
item.description: DeerFlow 2.0 hit #1 on GitHub Trending in February 2026. I explored its multi-layer memory architecture: within-session summarization, cross-session long-term storage, and isolated sub-agent contexts.

item.title: RAG vs. Memory in AI Systems: What Developers Need to Know in 2026
item.description: A practical guide to understanding the difference between retrieval-augmented generation and conversation memory, when to use each, and how to combine them effectively.

item.title: Short-Term Memory for AI Agents: What It Is, Why It Matters, and How to Implement It
item.description: A practical guide to understanding and implementing short-term memory for AI agents in 2026 — from context windows to sliding buffers to importance-weighted systems.

item.title: The BEAM Memory Benchmark: Why 1M Context Windows Fail Production AI Agents
item.description: The BEAM benchmark from ICLR 2026 shows that even 1M token context windows cannot solve the memory problem. Here's what this means for anyone building AI agents in production.

item.title: Why 1M Context Windows Are a Lie (And What Actually Works)
item.description: Context windows keep growing. Models keep forgetting. Here's the research proving that stuffing more tokens into your prompt isn't the same as memory, and what persistent memory architectures actually do differently.

item.title: The Memory Hierarchy in AI Systems: From Sensory Input to Semantic Knowledge
item.description: How modern AI agents borrow from cognitive science to build memory systems that persist, learn, and retrieve across sessions. A deep dive into the four-layer hierarchy that powers stateful AI.

item.title: The State of AI Agent Memory in 2026
item.description: A deep dive into how production AI systems handle memory in 2026 — the tiered architectures, the fragmentation crisis, the standardization push, and what is still fundamentally broken.

item.title: How Memory Works in Claude Code: A Deep Dive into Anthropic's Coding Agent
item.description: An in-depth exploration of how Claude Code stores, retrieves, and forgets information across sessions, and how to leverage its memory architecture for better workflows.

item.title: How Memory Works in HyperAgents: A Deep Dive into Meta's Self-Improving AI Architecture
item.description: An in-depth look at how HyperAgents, Meta's 2026 self-modifying AI framework, develop and manage memory systems autonomously — and why this changes everything for agent infrastructure.

item.title: Technical Writing for Engineers: The 80/20 Guide
item.description: Most engineering documentation fails for the same reasons. Here is what actually moves the needle.

item.title: LLM token budgets: a practical guide to cost control
item.description: Real numbers, real pricing, and concrete strategies for keeping your LLM spend predictable.

item.title: RAG Evaluation Metrics: What Actually Matters
item.description: A practical guide to RAGAs, recall, precision, and the metrics that separate production RAG systems from prototypes.

item.title: The 800ms Barrier: Profiling the Latency Chain of a Real-Time Gemini 3.1 Voice Agent
item.description: I built a sub-second latency voice assistant and profiled every millisecond of the Audio-to-Audio request/response loop on a MacBook Air M2. Here is the bottleneck analysis.

item.title: What Nobody Tells You About Error Handling in Production AI Agents
item.description: Hard-won lessons from running AI agents in production: the error patterns that actually break systems, and the patterns that fix them.

item.title: Context Engineering as Heap Management: Measuring Accuracy vs. KV Cache Eviction
item.description: VRAM is too expensive to waste on low-attention tokens. I benchmarked KV cache eviction strategies to treat LLM context like a managed heap, reaching 90% pruning with zero recall loss.

item.title: From 4 Minutes to 12 Seconds: A Quantitative Audit of the uv Package Manager
item.description: I benchmarked Astral uv against Poetry and pip on a 2026 AI dependency stack. Here is how Rust-native tooling reduces build times by 20x.

item.title: 100ms Vector Search in the Browser: PGlite vs. SQLite-vec Head-to-Head
item.description: I benchmarked PGlite and SQLite-vec on a MacBook Air M2 to find the fastest WASM vector database for local-first AI applications.

item.title: Beyond the Chatbox: Benchmarking Claude Code vs. Gemini CLI for Autonomous Repository Refactoring
item.description: I ran a head-to-head benchmark of Claude Code and Gemini CLI on an M2 Air (16GB), testing their ability to autonomously refactor legacy code and resolve race conditions.
